steps.title: Solve the equation
steps.content: Add $53$ to both sides: $$k^2=144.$$ Taking square roots gives $k=\pm 12$.

steps.title: Identify the positive solution
steps.content: Choose the positive root, $k=12$. The answer is (D) $12$.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice D is correct. Adding $53$ to each side of the given equation yields ${k}^{2}=144$. Taking the square root of each side of this equation yields $k=\pm12$. Therefore, the positive solution to the given equation is $12$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect. This is the positive solution to the equation ${k}^{2}-53=20,683$, not ${k}^{2}-53=91$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect. This is the positive solution to the equation ${k}^{2}-53=5,131$, not ${k}^{2}-53=91$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C is incorrect. This is the positive solution to the equation ${k}^{2}-53=1,391$, not ${k}^{2}-53=91$.
